body_txt: This item is available from the Trope Co. catalog Who needs an Artillery, Armored, Engineer, Mechanized, or Motorized Brigade when you can have a 100-metre tall humanoid robot with a glowing sword and a fist that fires off like a missile? There's no argument — ordinary vehicles have nothing on the cool factor of Mecha . And when they're really huge, they just get even cooler! Which is what really matters in the end, right? Mecha themselves are usually divided between the " Super Robot Genre " and the " Real Robot Genre ", depending on the kind of sci-fi, though there are as many different kinds of settings for mecha as there are genres of fiction. It should be noted that, while often referred to as robots, many mecha are functionally more like vehicles, controlled either remotely or from within by a pilot instead of being pre-programmed or autonomous. Mythology as a whole is also replete with artificial humans and similar automatons (eg. Talos , the great bronze automaton built by Hephaestus. Dr. Hell from Mazinger Z finding an army of Humongous Mecha on a Greek island is actually based on that legend), but special mention goes to Hindu Mythology . One of the three barriers the gods set to protect their elixir, the Amrita, was a robot with rotary saws for hands . For those of you wanting to create your own Humongous Mecha story, we've got you covered . While not a reality yet or likely to be... Japan is working on it. And so is the US. And these two have had the first real-world giant robot fight . It's worth noting that the amount of Friendly Fandoms in the genre is huge ; in fact, most hardcore fans describe themselves as "mecha fans" rather than belonging to any certain franchise fandom. Keep in mind that this trope is specifically for particularly humongous mecha, not just any robot big enough for a human to fit inside. For its medium- and small-sized equivalents, see Mini-Mecha and Powered Armor . Also see Mecha for the more generic, not-necessarily-humongous Super-Trope .         Marvel Comics Other
